Tests the Math.abs function to verify it returns the absolute value of a negative integer.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion test that verifies the functionality of the `Math.abs` method in JavaScript. The test checks if the absolute value of the negative integer `-123513745` is equal to `123513745`. This is a narrow functionality test, focusing specifically on ensuring that the `Math.abs` function correctly computes the absolute value of a given number. The use of `assert` indicates that this code is likely part of a test suite or a debugging process to validate the behavior of mathematical operations in a JavaScript environment.
